name: DelegatedDataContract Lifecycle Hooks
description: Extension points on DelegatedDataContract firing during transaction building (before-hooks) and after submission (after-hooks). Before-hooks are synchronous data transforms; after-hooks are async side-effect points. Existing: beforeCreate, beforeUpdate. Planned: afterCreate, afterUpdate.

name: beforeCreate hook
description: Synchronous transform hook called in txnCreatingRecord() after merging defaults+id+type+caller data, before datum construction. Receives (record: TLike, context: createContext<TLike>). Base class is passthrough. Subclasses override to normalize data for on-chain policy compliance.

name: beforeUpdate hook
description: Synchronous transform hook called in txnUpdatingRecord() after merging existing record with updated fields, before datum construction. Receives (record: TLike, context: updateContext<T>) where context includes original on-chain record. Base class is passthrough.

name: afterCreate / afterUpdate hooks (planned)
description: Planned async side-effect hooks firing after successful transaction submission. Unlike before-hooks (synchronous transforms), after-hooks would not affect the transaction result. Use cases: cache invalidation, notifications, logging. Requirements in DelegatedDataContract.reqts.md REQT-2.0.
